wikitext:
{{Stub}} {{Spoiler warning}} {{StS2|Ancients}} are mysterious beings that offer boons to the player at the start of each [[sts2:Acts|Act]]. Ancients generally have to be unlocked before being able to meet them during a run (see also: {{2|Timeline}}). There are a total of 8 Ancients in the game. Most Ancients have a pool of 10 boons (Neow has 20 and Darv has 12) from which 3 are randomly selected and offered to the player. Compared to the {{QueryLink|Relics|rarity:Boss|Boss Relics}} in Slay the Spire 1, the player no longer has the ability to skip the choice altogether and instead has to pick one of the options offered. == Act 1 ==
[[File:StS2 Map-Neow.png|class=elite-img|link=Slay_the_Spire_2:Neow]]
{{2|Neow}}
''I’ve... remade you....''
''...go... ..up.....''
== Act 2 ==
[[File:StS2 Map-Orobas.png|class=elite-img|link=Slay_the_Spire_2:Orobas]]
{{2|Orobas}}
''Puppet puppet!! All the way up here?! Take these, take take!''
[[File:StS2 Map-Pael.png|class=elite-img|link=Slay_the_Spire_2:Pael]]
{{2|Pael}}
''A puppet has arrived?''
''Can you check on Father? I’m too tired...''
[[File:StS2 Map-Tezcatara.png|class=elite-img|link=Slay_the_Spire_2:Tezcatara]]
{{2|Tezcatara}}
''Oh, a visitor! Do come in, dear!''
''What can Tezcatara do for you?''
== Act 3 ==
[[File:StS2 Map-Nonupeipe.png|class=elite-img|link=Slay_the_Spire_2:Nonupeipe]]
{{2|Nonupeipe}}
''I haven’t had a visitor in a millennia!''
[[File:StS2 Map-Tanx.png|class=elite-img|link=Slay_the_Spire_2:Tanx]]
{{2|Tanx}}
''SO WEAK! BUT YOU HAVE POTENTIAL!
USE THIS WEAPON!!''
[[File:StS2 Map-Vakuu.png|class=elite-img|link=Slay_the_Spire_2:Vakuu]]
{{2|Vakuu}}
''Give yourself to me and you will be feared as much as I.''
== Multiple Acts ==
[[File:StS2 Map-Darv.png|class=elite-img|link=Slay_the_Spire_2:Darv]]
{{2|Darv}}
''...where did I put... Oh!''
''Come to see my collection!? Take anything from that pile over there, put it to good use!''
''Act 2/3''
== Effective Probability == The Ancients that appear at the start of Acts 2 and 3 are selected with equal probability from two separate pools. Each pool includes all unlocked Ancients specific to that act, and may include Darv if he is unlocked. === Without Darv Unlocked === {| class="wikitable" |+ ! rowspan="2" |Ancient ! colspan="2" |{{2|Acts|Act 2}} ! rowspan="2" |{{2|Acts|Act 3}} |- !{{2|Orobas}} Not Unlocked !{{2|Orobas}} Unlocked |- |{{2|Orobas}} | - |33.33% | - |- |{{2|Pael}} |50% |33.33% | - |- |{{2|Tezcatara}} |50% |33.33% | - |- |{{2|Nonupeipe}} | - | - |33.33% |- |{{2|Tanx}} | - | - |33.33% |- |{{2|Vakuu}} | - | - |33.33% |} === With Darv Unlocked === When unlocked, {{2|Darv}} is added to the Act 2 pool 50% of the time, and is added to the Act 3 pool 50% of the time and only if he was not added to the Act 2 pool. Darv cannot be unlocked without first unlocking Orobas, so any time he is added to an act's pool there will be 4 Ancients in that pool, meaning he will appear in Act 2 in 1 out of 8 runs and in Act 3 in 1 out of 16 runs. If Darv appears in Act 2 he cannot appear in Act 3; on the other hand, if he did not appear in Act 2, he may still have been added to the Act 2 pool, and will only appear in Act 3 in 1 out of 14 runs after another Ancient appeared in Act 2. {| class="wikitable" |+ ! rowspan="2" |Ancient ! rowspan="2" |{{2|Acts|Act 2}} ! colspan="3" |{{2|Acts|Act 3}} ! rowspan="2" |Total |- !{{2|Acts|Act 2}} {{2|Darv}} !No {{2|Acts|Act 2}} {{2|Darv}} !Overall |- |{{2|Orobas}} |29.17% | - | - | - |29.17% |- |{{2|Pael}} |29.17% | - | - | - |29.17% |- |{{2|Tezcatara}} |29.17% | - | - | - |29.17% |- |{{2|Nonupeipe}} | - |33.33% |30.95% |31.25% |31.25% |- |{{2|Tanx}} | - |33.33% |30.95% |31.25% |31.25% |- |{{2|Vakuu}} | - |33.33% |30.95% |31.25% |31.25% |- |{{2|Darv}} |12.50% | - |7.14% |6.25% |18.75% |} [[Category:ToUpdate]]
